memoryArtifacts.headline: Tomb of Robert Bingham, Bishop of Salisbury, in Salisbury Cathedral
memoryArtifacts.description: The tomb of Robert located in the Salisbury Cathedral.  He was the Bishop of the cathedral from 1229-1246 until he died.

zh.lifeSummary: Bishop Robert Bingham是Ralph de Bingham Sr和Mrs. Bingham的兒子，於1164年出生在英國薩默塞特塞頓 賓厄姆三世。他於1222年與Lucy Turberville在英國英格蘭多瑟特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他於1240年在英國英格蘭維爾爾索爾茲伯里被列為canon of sarum。他去世於1246年12月2日在英國維爾爾索爾茲伯里享年82歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭維爾爾索爾茲伯里。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Bishop Robert Bingham是Ralph de Bingham Sr和Mrs. Bingham的儿子，于1164年出生在英国薩默塞特塞頓 賓厄姆三世。他于1222年与Lucy Turberville在英国英格兰多瑟特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他于1240年在英国英格兰維爾爾索爾茲伯里被列为canon of sarum。他去世于1246年12月2日在英国維爾爾索爾茲伯里享年82岁，埋葬在英国英格兰維爾爾索爾茲伯里。

nameMeanings.name: Bingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Dorset) and Irish (County Mayo): habitational name from Bingham (Nottinghamshire). The placename is probably from an Old English folk-name Bynningas (‘the people associated with a man named Bynna’), or possibly from an unattested Old English word bing ‘a kettle-shaped hollow’, + Old English hām ‘homestead’.
Irish (Ulster, of Scottish origin): altered form of   Bigham  .
American shortened and altered form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames such as   Bingenheimer  .

variants.name: Ingham
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): habitational name from any of the places called Ingham, particularly in Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Suffolk. The placename is from the Old English personal name Inga + hām ‘homestead’. In Yorkshire this name was sometimes confused with Bingham in the 18th century.
English (of Norman origin): variant of the obsolete Norman surname Engaine; see Gain .

variants.name: Bigham
meanings: Scottish (Wigtownshire) and Irish: habitational name from either Bigholm in Beith parish (Ayrshire) or Bigholms in Langholm parish (Dumfriesshire). The second element of the placename is from Older Scots holm (Old Norse holmr) ‘islet’, ‘raised land in a marsh’, ‘a stretch of low-lying land beside a river; a meadow’. The first element may be an unattested Older Scots bigge, a shortened form of bigging ‘building’. The name was taken to Ulster in the 17th and 18th centuries, where it was used interchangeably with Bingham .

variants.name: Bingenheimer
meanings: German and Jewish (from Germany): habitational name for someone from Bingenheim on the Eder river (Hesse). Compare   Bingham  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: Bishop Robert Bingham was born in 1164, in Sutton Bingham, Somerset, England as the son of Ralph de Bingham Sr and Mrs. Bingham. He married Lucy Turberville in 1222, in Dorset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. In 1240, his occupation is listed as canon of sarum in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. He died on 2 December 1246, in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, at the age of 82, and was buried in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Bishop Robert Bingham est né en 1164, à Sutton Bingham, Somerset, Angleterre, fils de Ralph de Bingham Sr et Mrs. Bingham. Il a épousé Lucy Turberville en 1222, à Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. En 1240, sa profession de son indiquée est canon of sarum à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 2 décembre 1246, à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 82 ans, et a été enterré à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Lui sposato Lucy Turberville nel 1222, nel Dorset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Nel 1240, la sua occupazione è indicata come canon of sarum nel Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. ja.lifeSummary: Bishop Robert Binghamは1164年にイギリスソメルセトスットオン ビンガムでRalph de Bingham SrとMrs. Binghamの息子として生まれた。1222年にイギリスイギリスドールセトでLucy Turbervilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。1240年，イギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーでの職業はcanon of sarumと記録されている。彼は1246年12月2日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーで82歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Bishop Robert Bingham(은)는 1164년 영국 서머세트 수턴 빙함에서 Ralph de Bingham Sr(와)과 Mrs. Bingham의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1222년 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트에서 Lucy Turberville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 1240년, 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 canon of sarum이었다. 그는 1246년 12월 2일 영국 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Bishop Robert Bingham nasceu em 1164, em Sutton Bingham, Somerset, Inglaterra filho de Ralph de Bingham Sr e Mrs. Bingham. Ele casou-se com Lucy Turberville em 1222, em Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Em 1240, sua profissão aparece como canon of sarum em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 2 de dezembro de 1246, em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, com 82 anos, e foi sepultado em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Bishop Robert Bingham родился в 1164 г. в местности суттон бингхам, сомирсет, Англия. Его родителей звали Ralph de Bingham Sr и Mrs. Bingham. Он женился на женщине по имени Lucy Turberville в 1222 г. в местности дорсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. В 1240 году он работал по профессии canon of sarum в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 2 декабря 1246 г. в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 82 лет и был похоронен в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Bishop Robert Bingham nació en 1164, en Sutton Bingham, Somerset, Inglaterra como hijo de Ralph de Bingham Sr y Mrs. Bingham. Se casó con Lucy Turberville en 1222, en Dorset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. En 1240, su ocupación registrada era canon of sarum en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió el 2 de diciembre de 1246, en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 82 años, y fue sepultado en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
